codeReview.summary: The original blocking regression from `6871d15` was that `persistHistory` stopped writing `contextTranscript` even though `history-session-loader.ts` still restores `session.contextTranscript` and workflow generation reads `this.contextHistory`. Fix commit `5d1dcc9` restores that field in `packages/extension/sidepanel/ui/history/history-persistence.ts`, so sessions saved after the fix once again round-trip the extra context needed by `loadSession()` and `generateWorkflowFromSession()`. The value being persisted comes from `contextHistory`, which the sidepanel already populates through normalized message creation/normalization paths, so this targeted change adequately addresses the original blocking failure. I did not find any remaining blocking code issues in this fix.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `refactor-worker` final-validation procedure is still easy to skip in practice: this fix session completed without running all required end-of-task validations from the skill.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/refactor-worker/SKILL.md:64-86,98-107,130` requires final `npm run test:integration` and `npm run build` runs before completion. The transcript skeleton for worker session `cb086a2c-51c5-49a8-ac4f-8c8a8c96d4c6` shows `npm run typecheck`, `npm run test:unit`, `npm run lint`, `npm run check:repo-standards`, and `wc -l`, but no `npm run test:integration` or `npm run build` before commit/handoff.

summary: Pass: this fix resolves the prior blocking history-persistence regression by restoring saved `contextTranscript`, which lets session reloads and workflow generation recover the full context again. I did not find any remaining blocking code issues in the targeted fix, though I noted the recurring validation-procedure gap in the worker skill usage.
